title: chrysocephalum podolepideum
intro: Chrysocephalum podolepideum, also known as Swamp Gum, is a shrubby perennial plant native to eastern Australia. Traditionally, Swamp Gum has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, wound healing, and respiratory problems. Swamp Gum is often prepared as an infusion or tincture, and can also be found in herbal preparations such as teas, salves, and poultices.
